description_long: This house features a rustic, medieval-inspired design with a thatched gable roof and exposed wooden beams accentuating the white plastered walls. The structure is elevated on a stone foundation, with a small, attached entryway that has its own sloped roof and a wooden door reinforced with metal bands. The windows are small, rectangular, and framed with dark wood, some with a slightly warped, hand-crafted appearance. The exterior walls display a mix of stone and brick at the base, transitioning to smooth plaster above, with decorative timber framing on the sides and rear. The roof is made of straw or reed, giving it a textured, natural look, and the overall form is compact and sturdy, typical of traditional European cottages. The house exudes a cozy, old-world charm, suitable for a village or rural urban setting.
description: A rustic, medieval-style house with a thatched gable roof, exposed timber framing, and a stone foundation. Features a small entryway, wooden windows, and a cozy, traditional cottage appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a small gabled roof, wooden door, and a single window to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting two windows, prominent timber framing, and the full length of the thatched roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single window, visible timber cross-bracing, and the thatched roof extending over the back wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing one window, extensive timber framing, and the side of the entryway structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Small gabled entryway with a wooden door, stone base, and no porch or steps, featuring a simple overhang.
